checks.name: prompt=none → code without rendering a login page
GET /favicon.ico → 200
client_credentials → access-only, sub == client_id
authorization_code → id + access + refresh
authorization code is single-use
PKCE S256 happy path
PKCE mismatch → invalid_grant (pkce), then code is burned
checks.actual: 400 invalid_grant (invalid_pkce: code_verifier does not compute to code_challenge from request); retry 400

checks.name: PKCE asymmetry: challenge without verifier → invalid_grant
refresh_token → fresh access_token, same sub, different jti
refresh_token unknown → invalid_grant
cross-issuer refresh → invalid_grant (different issuer)
checks.actual: 400 invalid_grant (refresh_token was issued by a different issuer)

checks.name: no rotation: same refresh redeems twice
password → id + access, NO refresh, sub == username
checks.expected: 200 id+access, no refresh, sub=bob; any pw

checks.name: jwt-bearer → access-only, claims kept, iss re-stamped
jwt-bearer missing assertion → invalid_request
jwt-bearer no scope anywhere → invalid_request
token-exchange → access-only, issued_token_type, aud, no scope
token-exchange without client auth → invalid_request (ClientAuthentication)
checks.actual: 400 invalid_request (request must contain some form of ClientAuthentication.)

checks.name: private_key_jwt structural rules (token-exchange)

detail.note: signature deliberately unverified; only structure enforced

checks.name: grant_type: blank → invalid_request, unknown → invalid_grant

default registered claims present and well-formed (client_credentials)
tid claim equals the issuer id

mint ≡ grant: minted token verifies and userinfo echoes sub
mint reserved issuer ('_mock') → 4xx problem

checks.name: scenario is one-shot AND issuer-matched
scenario list/clear: depth 2 with issuer fields, DELETE → 0
capture list: token request appears with method/path
capture take: bodyBase64 is byte-exact
capture take on empty → 404
self-isolation: control-plane calls are never captured
clock freeze: token iat pins to the frozen instant
clock advance flips token expiry (introspect + userinfo)
clock unfreeze: frozen=false and a fresh token verifies
reset: clears scenarios+captures, unfreezes, KEEPS keys
checks.actual: reset cleared state and preserved keys

checks.name: CORS on POST reflects Origin (not *) + credentials
OPTIONS preflight → 204 + Allow-Methods includes POST
OPTIONS preflight echoes request headers → Allow-Headers

detail.note: Access-Control-Request-Headers is a forbidden request-header name — the browser strips it on fetch, so the server has nothing to echo into Access-Control-Allow-Headers. Verified out-of-band with curl.

detail.note: the method is not allowed for this resource

reserved '_mock' prefix is not a usable issuer → 404
control-plane error is problem+json (garbage clock advance)

detail.note: control/infra errors use RFC-9457 problem+json, never the OAuth2 shape

manual.name: Interactive login page mints an id_token for the entered identity
manual.instructions: Click Start: a popup opens the login page at /{issuer}/authorize with prompt=login (scope openid profile, fresh state+nonce, redirect_uri=/static/callback.html). In the popup, enter username alice and Additional claims {"email":"alice@example.com"}, then Sign in. The popup lands on callback.html and posts the code back; Start exchanges it at /token and reports the id_token sub + email. PASS when sub is "alice" and email is "alice@example.com".

manual.name: response_mode=fragment returns code+state in the URL fragment
manual.instructions: Click Start: a popup opens /{issuer}/authorize with response_mode=fragment and a fresh state. With interactive login off the server auto-issues the code in the URL fragment; callback.html reads location.hash and posts it back. PASS when Start reports code + matching state arrived via the fragment. (No form to fill.)

manual.name: response_mode=form_post self-submits code+state to redirect_uri
manual.instructions: Click Start: capture is cleared, then a popup opens /{issuer}/authorize with response_mode=form_post and redirect_uri set to /{issuer}/userinfo. The self-submitting form POSTs code+state to /userinfo, which the control plane captures. Start takes that request and inspects its body. PASS when the captured /userinfo POST body contains "code=" and the generated state.

manual.name: Debugger completes a full authorize→token round-trip
manual.instructions: Click Start (opens /{issuer}/debugger in a new window) or open it yourself, then submit the pre-filled debugger form. PASS when the result page reads "Token exchange complete" and shows access_token / id_token / refresh_token.

manual.name: Cross-origin CORS (reflect-origin + credentials)
manual.instructions: In the config panel, switch Base URL to a DIFFERENT origin that reaches this same server (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:<port> when the page is on http://localhost:<port>), then click the Discovery section Run button. Every discovery check must still PASS: a readable cross-origin response is only possible because the server reflects the Origin (never *) and sets Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true. Switch Base URL back when done. Use Start to auto-probe the 127.0.0.1/localhost variant of this page origin.
